extractedFieldValues.current_value: Richard A. Cobb, a 66-year-old male, is being considered for home health admission due to his complex medical status following a recent discharge from Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital on April 9, 2026. His primary diagnosis involves multiple wounds, including a significant number of ulcers located on his right hip, right medial leg, right lateral knee, right foot, left knee, left heel, and bilateral heels. These wounds require consistent and skilled wound care management, which includes cleansing, application of specialized dressings, and monitoring for signs of infection or complications. Mr. Cobb's functional limitations are notable; he experiences reduced mobility and requires assistance for activities of daily living due to pain and the need for ongoing wound care. The skilled care required for his condition includes skilled nursing for wound management and ongoing assessment of his healing progress, as well as potentially physical therapy to aid in mobility and rehabilitation.
